issue_title: Viterbi 算法得到的是类似贪心算法的局部最优？
issue_body:
Wiki 上的 Python 算法针对的是又两个隐藏状态的特例，所以可以像贪心一样从 起点开始，不断向前选择，每次选择都没有后效性。

现在对于分词的问题，有四个隐藏状态，那么这个时候的 Viterbi 算法 要得到全局最优解应该只能从从后往前回溯得到。如果从前往后，每一步选择局部最优，得到的结果不是全局最优。

英文维基上的伪代码是全局最优的一个通用实现，Python 的不是。

在维基参考文献上看到的一个比较清晰的[介绍](http://www.kanungo.com/software/hmmtut.pdf) 24页。

![Viterbi 算法截图](http://wertion.github.io/pic/hmmtut_%E9%A1%B5%E9%9D%A2_24.png) 

我的一些理解哈，也可能不对，麻烦大家看一下吧。

issue_title: wraps does not convert default values for optional arguments
issue_body:
If I define a function that takes Quantity inputs and has an optional argument with a default value like so:

    def myfunc(x,y=1*ureg('meter')):
        return x + y

It works just fine with only the required argument

    >>> a = 1 * ureg.meter
    >>> myfunc(a)
    <Quantity(2, 'meter')>

But if I try to use [wrapping](http://pint.readthedocs.org/en/latest/wrapping.html) on it:

    @ureg.wraps('meter',('meter','meter'))
    def myfunc(x,y=1*ureg('meter')):
        return x + y

and I only supply the required argument, wraps fails to convert the optional argument to a magnitude, leading to a DimensionalityError:

    >>> a = 1*ureg.meter
    >>> myfunc(a)
    ...
    DimensionalityError: Cannot convert from 'meter' to 'dimensionless'

Looking at this issue with the debugger shows that the argument I supplied is converted into a magnitude by wraps, but the optional one is still a quantity. Of course everything works fine if both arguments are given:

    >>> a = 1*ureg.meter
    >>> myfunc(a,a)
    <Quantity(2, 'meter')>

Obviously this makes it difficult to use wraps with complex functions.

issue_title: HTTP "short body" exception for file transfers that exceed one packet
issue_body:
_From [tdea...@gmail.com](https://code.google.com/u/105413156194563035680/) on December 13, 2010 20:34:32_

What steps will reproduce the problem? 1. Download any file that spans multiple packets
2. Parse through the pcap file and look at the "Content-length" of the HTTP packet
3. Because the download is split up in multiple packets, the size in bytes is for the entire download rather than the HTTP packet What is the expected output? What do you see instead? I am not expecting to receive an exception What version of the product are you using? On what operating system? dpkt 1.7 Please provide any additional information below. My current fix is just to comment out lines 57 and 58 of http.py like:
\#if len(body) != n:
\#   raise dpkt.NeedData('short body (missing %d bytes)' % (n - len(body)))

If these are not commented out, I receive the "short body" exception

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=57_

issue_title: OS X backend not working when called from cron
issue_body:
It was [recently brought to my attention](https://github.com/n8henrie/pycookiecheat/issues/8) that `python-keyring` doesn't appear to be able to retrieve a password from the OS X Keychain when called from cron. Any ideas why?

I assumed it has to do with the keychain being locked, but I'm able to access the keychain from cron using the `security` program (which keyring uses as its default OS X backend), as long as my cron entry first unlocks the keychain, e.g. `security unlock-keychain -p MYPASS /path/to/login.keychain && security find-generic-password -a ACCOUNT -s SERVICE -g >> outfile.log`

issue_title: SSHTunnelForwarder.daemon_forward_servers is not respected
issue_body:
Opening this issue to get any thoughts before I do a PR.

Test case:

In ipython, construct a tunnel using:
```
tun = SSHTunnelForwarder((ssh_host, 22), ssh_username=user,
                              remote_bind_address=remote, local_bind_address=('127.0.0.1',),
                              ssh_pkey=ssh_key)
tun.daemon_forward_servers = True
tun.daemon_transport = True
tun.start()

In [12]: [(repr(t), t.isDaemon()) for t in threading.enumerate()]
Out[12]:
[('<_MainThread(MainThread, started 140736862168000)>', False),
 ('<HistorySavingThread(IPythonHistorySavingThread, started 123145316188160)>',
  True),
 ('<paramiko.Transport at 0xf68c048 (cipher aes128-ctr, 128 bits) (active; 1 open channel(s))>',
  True),
 ('<Thread(Srv-61825, started daemon 123145326698496)>', True),
 ('<Thread(Thread-282, started daemon 123145337208832)>', False)]
```

That last one should also be a daemon. The workaround is to set `_ThreadingForwardServer.daemon_threads=True`.

Questions:

* Is there a particular reason to have a global `DAEMON`?
  * It looks like public interface, but you can't update it once classes are initialized.
  * And, yuck, globals.
* Is there any benefit to having a separate `daemon_forward_servers` and `daemon_transport` field?

Proposal:
1. replace `DAEMON` with literal `False`
  * Still want a default to protect from crashes.
2. `_make_ssh_forward_server` sets `ssh_forward_server.daemon_threads = self.daemon_forward_servers`
3. Replace `SSHTunnelForwarder.daemon_*` with `.daemon`.

issue_title: isbntools fail under Python 3.7
multi get/set support
Duplicate field names in packet layer
issue_body:
Currently if there is a duplicate field name within a layer in the packet only one shows up within the layer object.

This problem occured when looking at something like GSM SMS where tshark reassembles the message fragments together in the last fragment. 

```xml
<field name="gsm_sms.sms_text" showname="SMS text: message 1" size="" pos="" show="" value=""/>
<field name="gsm_sms.sms_text" showname="SMS text: message 2" size="" pos="" show="" value=""/>
<field name="gsm_sms.sms_text" showname="SMS text: message 3" size="" pos="" show="" value=""/>
```
(I'm unable to share the actual XML I have in full so I've just written out the bit where the problem arises following the same structure but removing data)

I have managed to solve it by specifying tshark not to reassemble GSM_SMS packets using the argument: 
```python
pyshark.FileCapture(
    'file.pcap',
    override_prefs={'gsm_sms.reassemble': 'false'},
)
```
Is it worth changing the behaviour of the code to deal with issues like this or leave it up to the user to specify extra arguments to get around it. Not sure whether there are any cases where this can happen where extra arguments wont solve the issue.

issue_title: Jedi hangs for a long time with DataFrame containing timestamps
issue_body:
Hi! Neat tool, thanks for publishing it and maintaining it.

I'm using jedi `0.10.2`. Python `3.6`, and Jupyter Notebook `5` and ipython `6`.

**Quick summary**

* Completions take upwards of 30 seconds with a DataFrame with 1Mrow and 2 columns, if one of the columns is a timestamp.
* The runtime scales with the number of rows in the DataFrame.
* Waiting 30 seconds or more for completions is too long - during which time no other computation can take place.
* If you interrupt the kernel during jupyter doing the completion, the kernel dies - leading to dataloss and inconvenience (maybe this is a jupyter notebook or ipython bug?).
* It was tricky to figure out this was a jedi problem. It felt for a long time like a jupyter or ipython problem.
* Sending `KeyboardInterrupt` during a manual `Interpreter()` invocation reveals that jedi is running `df.values`, which takes many seconds to run for the aforementioned DataFrame (maybe this is a pandas bug?).

---

Since I've upgraded to the latest ipython and jupyter, I've experienced regular poor performance and hanging of the kernel at odd times during everyday use. I wasn't experiencing this before. I currently suspect jedi may be the culprit. What I observe is that I'll be typing something in, and maybe if I press tab, the kernel becomes unresponsive. I see 100% CPU usage. The hang lasts at least 30 seconds, perhaps longer. If I try to interrupt the kernel in order to get a backtrace, the ipython kernel dies.

I was able to reproduce the problem with the following - tab completing on a dataframe with 1Mrow, and 2 columns: 1 timestamp, and one plain integer:

```python
%%time
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

from jedi import Interpreter

rows = 1000000

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "foobar": np.random.randint(int(1e18), size=rows).astype("<M8[ns]"),
    "baz": [1] * rows,
})
s = "df.hello"
print(Interpreter(s, [{'df': df}] , line=1, column=len(s)).completions())
```

This chunk of code runs for more than 15 seconds. The runtime increases if you increase the number of rows.

I've searched jupyter, ipython, ipykernel and jedi's issue trackers and didn't find anyone else having the problem, so I'm wondering if it is something peculiar to my DataFrame. I also wonder if the issue is quite hard to attribute to jedi, so maybe people are experiencing the issue and failing to know where to report it. I had difficulty making a reproducing case I could share, since it requires both a timestamp column and a non-timestamp column (likely the mixed types are what make `df.values` slow, I guess).

issue_title: completion error for groupby when DataFrame is created in a function
issue_body:
The first script runs OK:
```python
import jedi
good_source = \
"""from pandas import read_stata
bar_var = read_stata('bad_file')
bad_var.groupby(['aa'])['bb'].mean()
"""
good_ss = jedi.Script(source=good_source, line=3, column=31)
good_ss.completions()
```

However, this script produces errors on both Windows 10 and macOS:
```python
import jedi
bad_source = \
"""from pandas import read_stata
def bad_fun():
	bar_var = read_stata('bad_file')
	return bar_var	
bad_var = bad_fun()
bad_var.groupby(['aa'])['bb'].mean()
"""
bad_ss = jedi.Script(source=bad_source, line=6, column=31)
bad_ss.completions()
```

Is this expected behavior or an open issue? I first encountered this when using `elpy` on `emacs`. I'm using `pandas 0.22.0` and `jedi 0.12.0`. Thanks for the awesome package.

issue_title: Behavior inconsistent between versions on pypi and sourceforge
issue_body:
# Code
I am attempting to make an example service with this code:
```import win32serviceutil
import win32service
import win32event
import servicemanager
import socket
import time
import logging
# Logs to the temp directory under C
logging.basicConfig(
    filename='C:\\Temp\\allamericanregress.log',
    level=logging.DEBUG,
    format='[allamericanregress-service] %(levelname)-7.7s %(message)s'
)

class AllAmericanRegressService(win32serviceutil.ServiceFramework):
    _svc_name_ = "AllAmericanRegress"
    _svc_display_name_ = "AllAmericanRegress"

    def __init__(self, args):
        win32serviceutil.ServiceFramework.__init__(self, args)
        self.stop_event = win32event.CreateEvent(None, 0, 0, None)
        socket.setdefaulttimeout(60)
        self.stop_requested = False

    # How to respond to service stop command
    def SvcStop(self):
        self.ReportServiceStatus(win32service.SERVICE_STOP_PENDING)
        win32event.SetEvent(self.stop_event)
        logging.info('Stopping service ...')
        self.stop_requested = True

    # When launched by service control manager, log brief intro
    def SvcDoRun(self):
        servicemanager.LogMsg(
            servicemanager.EVENTLOG_INFORMATION_TYPE,
            servicemanager.PYS_SERVICE_STARTED,
            (self._svc_name_, '')
        )
        self.main()

    def main(self):
        logging.info(' ** Doing things ** ')
        return


if __name__ == '__main__':
    win32serviceutil.HandleCommandLine(AllAmericanRegressService, None, ["AllAmericanRegressService","--startup=auto", "install"])
    win32serviceutil.HandleCommandLine(AllAmericanRegressService, None, ["AllAmericanRegressService", "start"])
    # win32serviceutil.HandleCommandLine(AllAmericanRegressService)
```

# Pypi version behavior

When installing pypiwin32 via pip and executing this file from administrator command prompt I get an error and my service never starts.

Installation:
```
C:\Users\chris>pip install pypiwin32                                                                                    Collecting pypiwin32                                                                                                      Using cached pypiwin32-220-cp36-none-win_amd64.whl                                                                    Installing collected packages: pypiwin32                                                                                Successfully installed pypiwin32-220     
```

Executing:


```
Installing service AllAmericanRegress
Changing service configuration
Service updated
Starting service AllAmericanRegress
Error starting service: The service did not respond to the start or control request in a timely fashion.
```

# Sourceforge version behavior

However, when installing via the installer available at https://sourceforge.net/projects/pywin32/files/pywin32/Build%20221/pywin32-221.win-amd64-py3.6.exe/download I get no errors and the services is instantiated ad executes as expected and I get this output:
```
Installing service AllAmericanRegress
Changing service configuration
Service updated
Starting service AllAmericanRegress
```

# Question
Why do these version behave differently? Alternatively, how can the installer be executed headlessly?

So I tried to use a cache file to store the data. You'll have to take my word for it, but the file was properly stored and I was able to open it:

```python
    with open( 'cache' , 'wb') as f:
        f.write( urlopen( imageURL ).read() )
    bot.sendPhoto( chat, open( 'cache', 'rb' ) )
```
This gave me the exact same error. However, appending '.jpg' to the end of the cached file name worked and the image was properly posted in the chat.

So my question is 'How do I post a photo without caching the data in a file with an image extension?'

issue_title: Weird classification for Latvian & ex-Yougoslavian languages
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed some weird classification problems for Latvian, the text (a subtitle) being typically classified as Luxemburgish (!). As the codes for the two languages follow each other, I was wondering whether this might be due to a bug (in the training data or the classifier)? 

Bosnian, Croatian and Serbian are also difficult to classify (these languages being very close, and until recently often classified as one single language). However, it seems that the classification of Croatian "overweights" the two other languages -- if I try to classify Bosnian and Serbian subtitles, I typically get Croatian as only possible language.

issue_title: time.strftime returns unexpected values
issue_body:
Freezegun mocked strftime seems dependent to the system timezone.
```python
# strftime.py
import time, freezegun                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
with freezegun.freeze_time("1970-01-01 00:00:01"):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
    print(time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z')) 
```
```bash
$ env TZ=CET python strftime.py
1970-01-01 01:00:01
 
$ env TZ=UTC python strftime.py
1970-01-01 00:00:01

$ env TZ=CET faketime "1970-01-01 00:00:01" python -c "import time; print(time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z'))"                                                                                                                                                                                                          
1970-01-01 00:00:01 CET

$ env TZ=UTC faketime "1970-01-01 00:00:01" python -c "import time; print(time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z'))"
1970-01-01 00:00:01 UTC
```

I think strftime should not apply timezone shifts, and behave like libfaketime does. What do you think?

Also the `%Z` parameters differs from libfaketime but I do not know what I am thinking about this :)

issue_title: Null terminator for SSID in join_ibss
issue_body:
The Problem is that when I tried to connect the mesh network via `join_ibss`, the SSID will reveal ending with `\x00` at the terminal by using `iw dev`.

The null terminators seem useful for sending some of the messages to netlink in the `pyroute2/netlink/__init__.py`, but this is not the case for the names. So, I come up with a ["hacky" solution](https://github.com/creasyw/pyroute2/commit/2ba8779dc972338229257a2b842e69a9bf489997).

Please let me know how I can improve it, then I will make the pull request for it.

Thanks a lot!

issue_title: Idea: Provide a command line tool
issue_body:
First of all, i don't need this, I'm just proposing this on the "nice to have" level.
I think it could help anymarkup to gain more glory.

The ultimate `/usr/bin/anymarkup`
------------------------------------------

Reads from stdin or file, outputs to stdout or file. Has `--from` and `--to` optional format switches, guesses the format otherwise based on file extension and/or provided content. (Actually when writing to stdout, `--to` needs to be mandatory).

All the logic is already there, all this needs is a nice entry point and click.

Imagine the glory of this tool, you could do:

issue_title: Odd-length hex values (leading zeros dropped)
issue_body: I ran into an issue when testing this package against [my implementation of SRP in Swift](https://github.com/Bouke/SRP). Eventually I found that this package uses `'%x' % val` inside `def hex_from(val):` to convert (big) integers to hexadecimal. This method doesn't include any leading zero to make the hex output even length. For now I've worked around this [[example](https://github.com/Bouke/SRP/blob/master/remote.py)], but wanted to raise some awareness about this, as having odd-length hex values confused me a little.

issue_title: Tablesorter resorting in combination with setInterval and CSS3 animation
issue_body:
I make an application for betting where I load new JSON data on every 3s and if it is necessary, add, delete or update some rows. I use tablesorter plugin and it's working great, but I have one specific case. If there is a result change, I add "highlight" CSS class so that row will pulse about 8 seconds in orange using CSS3 animation and after completing of animation, that class is removed. This works perfectly, but if I use tablesorter resort option, that animation starts again on every 3s and practically never ends, because it lasts longer than set interval (8s is less than 3s). So, if I put resort option to false, the problem with animation doesn't exist and it will be continuous until it's finished, but I would like to use resort option on every update.

To better explain the situation, here is a JSFiddle https://jsfiddle.net/savicmi/h9gpqrwn/15/ that demonstrates the problem. If it is added a row with Basketball in it, that row's background will pulse. It seems that tablesorter redraws table's body if it is resort == true, and probably it is the reason for starting CSS3 animation from the beginning instead of continuous duration up to the end of an animation. How can this be solved?

issue_title: inconsistent application of sourceTime in Calendar.parseDT
issue_body:
Using the parseDT method, some relative strings use the provided source time for comparison, others use the actual current time:

```
In [3]: naiveDtm = datetime.datetime(2015, 6, 9, 1, 17, 0)

Out[8]: (datetime.datetime(2015, 6, 9, 20, 6, 49), 2)
```

parsedatetime is considering "now" to be the current time even when a sourceTime is provided. "ago" is using the sourceTime as a reference.

issue_title: Incorrect urljoin when getting couchdb user
issue_body:
It appears that replacing urljoin by urllib.parse.urljoin causes a problem when getting a user from couchdb. I have found that it is caused by urllib handling the colon in typical user id e.g. org.couchdb.user:username

```
>>> from urlparse import urljoin
>>> urljoin('http://example.com', 'org.couchdb.user:username')
'org.couchdb.user:username'
```

The same applies to python 3.x

This leads to 
```
requests.exceptions.InvalidSchema: No connection adapters were found for 'org.couchdb.user:username'
```

To me it looks like urllib problem as I would say a colon is an allowed character.

issue_title: Invalid "part" argument results in corrupted repo under certain configs
issue_body:
As mentioned in #89, I managed to replicate this issue in a very simply repo. In a fresh `git init`'d folder, add and commit these two files:

.bumpversion.cfg:
```
[bumpversion]
current_version = 1.0.0
commit = True
tag = True
tag_name = {new_version}

[bumpversion:file:test.txt]
search = tmtpds.git@{current_version}
replace = tmtpds.git@{new_version}
```

test.txt:
```
This is a test, this is only a test.

tmtpds.git@1.0.0
```

If you then execute something like `bumpversion blah --dry-run --verbose`, using any invalid "part" argument besides `major`, `minor`, or `patch`, you'll see this output:

```
$ bumpversion giggle --dry-run --verbose
Reading config file .bumpversion.cfg:
[bumpversion]
current_version = 1.0.0
commit = True
tag = True
tag_name = {new_version}

[bumpversion:file:test.txt]
search = tmtpds.git@{current_version}
replace = tmtpds.git@{new_version}


Parsing version '1.0.0' using regexp '(?P<major>\d+)\.(?P<minor>\d+)\.(?P<patch>\d+)'
Parsed the following values: major=1, minor=0, patch=0
Attempting to increment part 'giggle'
Values are now: major=1, minor=0, patch=0
Dry run active, won't touch any files.
Parsing version '1.0.0' using regexp '(?P<major>\d+)\.(?P<minor>\d+)\.(?P<patch>\d+)'
Parsed the following values: major=1, minor=0, patch=0
New version will be '1.0.0'
Asserting files test.txt contain the version string:
Found 'tmtpds.git@1.0.0' in test.txt at line 2: tmtpds.git@1.0.0
Would change file test.txt:
--- a/test.txt
+++ b/test.txt
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
 This is a test, this is only a test.
 
-tmtpds.git@1.0.0
+tmtpds.git@tmtpds.git@1.0.0
Would write to config file .bumpversion.cfg:
[bumpversion]
current_version = 1.0.0
commit = True
tag = True
tag_name = {new_version}

[bumpversion:file:test.txt]
search = tmtpds.git@{current_version}
replace = tmtpds.git@{new_version}


Would prepare Git commit
Would add changes in file 'test.txt' to Git
Would add changes in file '.bumpversion.cfg' to Git
Would commit to Git with message 'Bump version: 1.0.0 → 1.0.0'
Would tag '1.0.0' in Git
```

Not only is it performing a commit without incrementing the version number, but it's also *corrupting* test.txt by repeating "tmtpds.git@" within the file. And if you're doing this on an existing repo with commits and tags and don't do `--dry-run`, you'll get an error from git: `fatal: tag '1.0.0' already exists`.

The cause of this appears to be that `bumpversion` doesn't sanitize its "part" argument, accepting any old string it's given, rather than throwing an error when it gets something it doesn't understand.

I'm not at all sure where that corruption is coming from, though. That's an entirely different problem than simple argument sanitization.

issue_title: enhancement: pre-release support
issue_body:
A release workflow we follow is:
- from 2.4.3 we may create and of the following pre-releases: 2.4.4-rc1, 2.5.0-rc1, 3.0.0-rc1
- from a prerelease we either increment the last digit for a new prerelease, or drop the -rc? for the release

A similar workflow would use -alpha, -beta, -gamma as the pre-release identifiers.

This is only currently supported by `bump2version` using the `--new-version` flag, but could be supported by `--prerelease` and `--release` flags. (I know, please submit a PR ... :))

issue_title: Command Line utility
issue_body:
Would a command-line utility be useful? For my research I often need to geocode many addresses, usually in tabular format, so it would be excellent to have something like this, that accepts input as filenames or from STDIN.

    $ geocode myfile.txt google
    # Full json response, one json document per line
    ...
    $ cat myfile.txt | geocode osm
    # Full json response, one json document per line
    ...

Receiving a json response rather than selected fields is convenient, as it keeps the complexities associated to the document schemas (which are different for each service) out of the utility. The json responses can later be parsed easily, for instance with utilities like jq.

    $ cat myfile.txt | geocode google | jq [.lat,.lng,.country] -c
    [37.4219985,-122.0839544,"United States"]
    ...

As I need it for my work, I can take care of developing this utility. Do you think this might be useful for the project?

issue_title: Polygons not showing in QGIS 2.18
issue_body:
Shapefiles with shapeType = shapefile.POLYGON don't show up. Same for shapefiles generated with shapeType =shapefile.POLYLINE. Both in GQGIS 2.18

As it turned out after several hours searching for the cause:

POLYGON shapefile parts have to be writting with shapele.Writer.poly(parts=...)) and
POLYLINE shapefile parts have to be written with shapefile.Writer.line(parts= ... )) 

The documentation says that you're free to choose either line(..) or poly(...) to write the coordinates, and indeed both shapefiles are read by QGIS without complaints, and QGIS knows the extent of the shapfiles, but they don't display unless they are generated by shapefile.Writer by the correct function.

Theo Olsthoorn 180610

issue_title: Add method to get URL from WikiLink
issue_body: Figuring out either the absolute or relative URLs of a WikiLink is non-trivial. The code for it in mediawiki (starting at, say, https://github.com/wikimedia/mediawiki/blob/wmf/1.26wmf24/includes/Title.php#L1679) is hideous and requires many parameters (the link may even [depend upon whether the server is running IIS 7](https://github.com/wikimedia/mediawiki/blob/c42f06642091e1cb1142066b0a52a69a0c7e783d/includes/GlobalFunctions.php#L434), bizarrely), making it difficult to figure out the logic. It would be nice to port this to `mwparserfromhell`, taking all the necessary bits of information that can't be inferred from the markup as parameters.

issue_title: add support for ignoring limit checks based on resource name / dynamodb table name.
issue_body:
### Feature Description

It would be nice to have ability to skip certain checks based on the limit and resource name (or table name, in case of dynamo), to have the ability to suppress warnings on subsequent checks.

### Use Cases

Currently we get the following warning:
DynamoDB/Global Secondary Indexes  (limit 5) WARNING: my-table-name=4

We know that this table is at 4, and will remain that way. If someone modifies the table, by adding 2 more secondary indexes, we are fine that the update fails. We want to "whitelist/suppress" this alert for this table permanently. 
But we still would like to maintain ability to have this alert for other/new tables, when they appear on the same aws account/region, and start to reach the limit of secondary indexes.

### Testing Assistance

i'm able to test this feature.

issue_title: Reversion not working on Celery or Shell
issue_body:
Reversion is not working on Shell or Celery, after debugging I found that the admin files are called, the signals are there but the Reversion Context is deactivated and that's why it's not working.
I had to implement this code to be able to use Reversion on Celery:

```python
def task_pre_run_handler(**kwargs):
    """Activate Reversion Context Manager"""
    reversion.revision_context_manager.start()


def task_post_run_handler(**kwargs):
    """Deactivate Reversion Context Manager"""
    reversion.revision_context_manager.end()


task_prerun.connect(task_pre_run_handler)
task_postrun.connect(task_post_run_handler)
```

issue_body:
Imagine I have a base `urls.py`:

```python
urlpatterns = patterns(
    '',

    url(r'^(?P<project_slug>[a-z0-9-_]{1,256})/signup/',
        include('myapp.signup.urls',
                namespace='signup',
                app_name='signup'),),
)

The URLs inside of `myapp.signup.urls` are defined as:

```python
urlpatterns = patterns(
    '',

    url(r'^create/?$',
        view=SignupConfigCreateView.as_view(),
        name='create'),
)
```

The result (relevant part only) of the JS is:

```
url_patterns = [
                [
                    'signup:create', ['(?P<project_slug>[a-z0-9-_]{1,256})/signup/create', []]
                ],
        ];
```

This is not correct. 

If I leave out the namespace param in the include call as such:

```python
urlpatterns = patterns(
    '',

    url(r'^(?P<project_slug>[a-z0-9-_]{1,256})/signup/',
        include('myapp.signup.urls',
                app_name='signup'),),
)
```

the result JS is correct:

```
url_patterns = [
                [
                    'create', ['%(project_slug)s/signup/create', ['project_slug']]
                ]
        ];
```

issue_title: tldextract does not parse private domains
issue_body:
tldextract state that 
> tldextract accurately separates the gTLD or ccTLD (generic or country code top-level domain) from the registered domain and subdomains of a URL.

It is true but there is important information omitted from the claims.   
Apparently, tldextract DOES NOT parse all the domain inside the public suffix list, i.e.  private domain are IGNORED. 

```
import tldextract
domains = [
"subdomain.test.beep.pl",
"subdomain.test.ap-southeast-2.compute.amazonaws.com",
"subdomain.test.za.org"
]
for domain in domains:
    result = tldextract.extract(domain)
    print(result) 
```

results : 
```
ExtractResult(subdomain='subdomain.test', domain='beep', suffix='pl')
ExtractResult(subdomain='subdomain.test.ap-southeast-2.compute', domain='amazonaws', suffix='com')
ExtractResult(subdomain='subdomain.test', domain='za', suffix='org')
```

It seems tldextract looking for some thing like // ===END ICANN DOMAINS=== and skip those. 

A note is required to warn the user on omission of the private domains parsing. Or tldextract need additional function to parse private domains.

issue_title: Difference between SGP4 1.4 and Pyhem
issue_body:
Hello,

I started to play with tracking the ISS space station.
Have approached  it with Pyhem and SGP4 1.4.
When I do the calculation I get a different answer for the longitude.
I have checked the results with a tracker found on the internet, and the value of the calculation with Pyephem are the same!

I use the ECEF routine to convert the (x,y,z) to lat,lon and alt.


Thanks for any support,or help.

Jan Kromhout
Hellevoetsluis-NL

import urllib.request
from sgp4.earth_gravity import wgs84
from sgp4.io import twoline2rv
from datetime import datetime
from math import sqrt, atan2, sin, cos, pi
import ephem 

def ecef(x, y, z):  #Convert (x,y,z) to (lat,lon,alt)
    a = 6378137
    e = 8.1819190842622e-2
    b = sqrt(a ** 2 * (1 - e ** 2))
    ep = sqrt((a ** 2 - b ** 2) / (b ** 2))
    p = sqrt(x ** 2 + y ** 2)
    th = atan2(a * z, b * p)
    lon = atan2(y,x)
    lat = atan2((z + ep ** 2 * b * sin(th) ** 3), (p - e ** 2 * a * cos(th) ** 3))
    n = a / sqrt(1 - e ** 2 * sin(lat) ** 2)
    alt = p / cos(lat) - n
    lon = lon % (2 * pi)
    return lat*180/pi, lon*180/pi, alt

def update_tle():
  data=[]
  #Schrijf data tijdelijk in file 
  urllib.request.urlretrieve('http://www.celestrak.com/NORAD/elements/stations.txt','test.dat')
  f=open('test.dat','r')
  data=f.read()
  data=data.split('\n')
  return data[0],data[1],data[2] #Gegevens International Space Station

print('Read stations data')
name,line1,line2=update_tle()

print(name)
print(line1)
print(line2)

satellite = twoline2rv(line1, line2, wgs84)
now=datetime.utcnow()
print ('UTC time        ',now)
print('Calculate with sgp4 1.4')
position, velocity = satellite.propagate(now.year,now.month,now.day,now.hour,now.minute,now.second)
lat,lon,alt=ecef(position[0]*1000,position[1]*1000,position[2]*1000)
speed=sqrt(velocity[0]**2+velocity[1]**2+velocity[2]**2)*3600
print('epochyr                  ',satellite.epochyr)
print('epochdays                ',satellite.epochdays)
print('jdsatepoch               ',satellite.jdsatepoch)
print('epoch                    ',satellite.epoch)
print('position (km)            ',position)
print('velocity (km/s)          ',velocity)
print('orbital speed (km/h)     ',speed)
print()
print('Position of ISS at time  ',now)
print('Latitude (deg)          ',lat)
print('Longitude(deg)          ',lon)
print('Height                   ',alt)

home = ephem.Observer()
home.lon        = '52'   # +E
home.lat        = '05'      # +N

iss= ephem.readtle(name, line1, line2)
iss.compute(now)
lat=ephem.degrees(iss.sublat)
lon=ephem.degrees(iss.sublong)
print('Calculate with Ephem')
print('Latitude (deg)           ',float(lat)*180/pi)
print('Longitude (deg)          ',float(lon)*180/pi)

issue_title: About renaming the python-socketio library
issue_body:
Hi,
I'm currently packaging python-socketio for debian and I'm facing the following problem : 
there's already a package named python-socketio in Debian : 

Debian package : https://packages.debian.org/unstable/python-socketio
Upstream project : https://github.com/abourget/gevent-socketio

I could renamed you package's name in debian, but your python library would still want to install in 
/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/socketio which is already used by package python-socketio from gevent-socketio.
So the python library name "socketio" is the problem.
From the few that I read, your library does not provide the same interface as gevent-socketio, so I can't
make both an alternative to each other in debian with a "Conflicts" keyword, meaning that users can install one package at a time in the filesystem and use them indifferently as they provide the same interface.
According to Github metric, gevent-socketio as 999stars and python-socketio has 89, which could mean that less projects are impacted by your python-socketio at the moment (I don't have other metric :) so I could actually be wrong here of course)
My question is, what would you think of renaming "socketio" and maybe "python-socketio" so that both
projects don't collide anymore in the python libraries namespace ?
Thanks for your thoughts on this,

Fred

issue_title: Tests fail when Python interpreter is not ‘python’
issue_body:
When the operating system command ‘python’ does not invoke the expected Python interpreter, the unit tests fail. This is because the tests assume that a subprocess, invoking the ‘python’ command, will correctly run the code for testing.

When this assumption is not true (for example, when the Python interpreter is installed to a non-standard location) the unit tests fail incorrectly.

                  ['softmax_label'], [(n,)],
                  [lambda s: np.random.randint(0, num_classes, s)])

```

## Steps to reproduce
Executed all the cells in the tutorial up to 41.

## What have you tried to solve it?
This is the correct implementation that makes the `fit` function to train correctly a model (the issue lies in the `provide_data/label` fields that don't use the `DataDesc` object:

```
class SimpleIter(mx.io.DataIter):
    def __init__(self, data_names, data_shapes, data_gen,
                 label_names, label_shapes, label_gen, num_batches=10):
        self._provide_data = [mx.io.DataDesc(name=name,shape=shape,layout='NTC') for name,shape in zip(data_names, data_shapes)]
        self._provide_label = [mx.io.DataDesc(name=name,shape=shape,layout='NTC') for name,shape in zip(label_names, label_shapes)]
        self.num_batches = num_batches
        self.data_gen = data_gen
        self.label_gen = label_gen
        self.cur_batch = 0

    def __iter__(self):
        return self

    def reset(self):
        self.cur_batch = 0

    def __next__(self):
        return self.next()

    @property
    def provide_data(self):
        return self._provide_data

    @property
    def provide_label(self):
        return self._provide_label

    def next(self):
        if self.cur_batch < self.num_batches:
            self.cur_batch += 1
            data = [mx.nd.array(g(d[1])) for d,g in zip(self._provide_data, self.data_gen)]
            label = [mx.nd.array(g(d[1])) for d,g in zip(self._provide_label, self.label_gen)]
            

            return mx.io.DataBatch(
                data,
                label,
                provide_data=self._provide_data,
                provide_label=self._provide_label
            )
        else:
            raise StopIteration
```

issue_title: Collection not filtering integer field
issue_body:
I have an integer field and when I try to create a filter on it it didn't work.

Looking at p.a.querystring code I see that the related operators don't work:
* plone.app.querystring.operation.int.is
* plone.app.querystring.operation.int.lessThan
* plone.app.querystring.operation.int.largerThan

They are mapped to these methods respectively:
* [plone.app.querystring.queryparser._equal](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.querystring/blob/1052e94636eaf750c747d619c730adaf45ef2082/plone/app/querystring/queryparser.py#L64)
* [plone.app.querystring.queryparser._lessThan](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.querystring/blob/1052e94636eaf750c747d619c730adaf45ef2082/plone/app/querystring/queryparser.py#L96)
* [plone.app.querystring.queryparser._largerThan](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.querystring/blob/1052e94636eaf750c747d619c730adaf45ef2082/plone/app/querystring/queryparser.py#L86)

And these methods don't convert the value to int (because they are used for other string based types).

Also I tested the plone.app.querystring.operation.selection.is and it fail too with an int field.

Looking at the [tests of these methods](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.querystring/blob/1052e94636eaf750c747d619c730adaf45ef2082/plone/app/querystring/tests/testQueryParser.py#L247), you clearly see that it is not testing every kind of data.

My suggestions are to create new methods just for int fields (doing type cast) and to add a new operator for int fields plone.app.querystring.operation.intselection.is (something like this)

issue_title: Should primitives be encoded with repr after reaching max_depth
issue_body:
When max_depth is reached, or when make_refs is unset and the object has already been seen, the data is returned with repr(), in order to stop recursing or break a cycle. For unicode objects this results in a "u'text data'" in the JSON output. Given that primitives have no possibility of continuing a recursion or cyclical reference, would it not be better to return the object?

e.g. instead of 
`if max_reached or (not self.make_refs and id(obj) in self._objs):`
Perhaps:
`if (max_reached or (not self.make_refs and id(obj) in self._objs)) and not util.is_primitive(obj):`
or 
`if (max_reached and not util.is_primitive(obj)) or (not self.make_refs and id(obj) in self._objs):`
Is there any reason within _flatten_obj() to not check utils.is_primitive and simply return the object without calling _get_flattener()?

issue_title: Create smooth normals respecting edges
issue_body:
It would be nice, if the normal generator would have an option to respect "real" edges. Of course there is always an edge between two triangles, because of the discrete nature of a mesh. But if the angle between neighbour triangles is 90° or above, the normals should not be smoothly change from one triangle to the next one. Maybe there could be a threshold to detect edges. Whenever the angle between two neighbour triangles is above the threshold, the edge between them is considered a "real" edge.

This implies that the vertices at the affected edge can not be joined (if this flag is set also), because the vertex normals at the same position have to differ. To "repair" the neighbourhood, we could insert "virtual" triangles with zero width at the edge connecting the neighbours.

issue_title: Find a replacement for icebox
issue_body:
Icebox does not work anymore, since the new calculators are not using postgres. In particular the loss map and curve visualization is already broken. We should replace it with some other technology.
Alternatively, one could replace the queries in `openquake.server.tasks.DBINTERFACE` with an equivalent procedure reading from the datastore and writing in the icebox database. However, that would not solve the shortcomings of the current icebox. It is better to replace icebox altogether and to move the visualization part in the engine server so that we can have a graphical user interface working on the user machines.

issue_title: getrusage(rusage_self).ru_maxrss is compared against 0 but should always be non-negative
issue_body:
I'm filing this issue my progress so far with a bug but it's currently incomplete. I am currently receiving the message "worker unable to determine memory usage" in my log. This is logged here: https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/cfeac80dfd9136924af066587942c360e9fb67d8/billiard/pool.py#L385 as a consequence of getrusage(rusage_self).ru_maxrss being < 0 but I am having trouble seeing how this is possible. I certainly don't think negative values are intended as error values (at least on Linux) as the current code seems to assume.

To explain why it shouldn't be possible we can look at the journey of ru_maxrss:
1. It goes from here https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/84787c572d402644dca4874aba73324d9f8e3948/kernel/sys.c#L1547 where it's an unsigned long (32 bits or 64 bits usually)
2. To here https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/84787c572d402644dca4874aba73324d9f8e3948/kernel/sys.c#L1613 ; https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/9256d5a308c95a50c6e85d682492ae1f86a70f9b/include/uapi/linux/resource.h#L26 where it's a __kernel_long_t which is signed long or signed long long depending on the arch (but again, probably 32 bits or 64bits)
3. Then it gets converted into a Python type here: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/2.7/Modules/resource.c#L89

The only way it could end up negative as far as I can see is by overflowing into the sign bit of a 32 bit signed integer, but in this case the RSS would have to be to be larger than 2**31-1 kilobytes which is >2TB. I don't have any processes this big.

issue_title: By default delay_exponential should not be random
issue_body:
The default behavior for retries includes a [random base](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/c632931a6cc5eab4113c976d430bcb9c059f829f/botocore/retryhandler.py#L39), default retry behavior for all services is to use `rand` base.

## Issues

### Issue 1

growth_factor makes no sense in the case when `rand` is used. Say at first you get value `base=0.7` and wait for `0.7` seconds, if next time you get `0.01` - you will wait for `0.02`. 

### Issue 2

The base in the range of `[0, 1)` can be very high. In case of lambda functions, when you pay for milliseconds, sleeping for example for 700 ms does not make any sense, and it is a lot of machine time, so I just don't see any reason for a service to wait so long before the first retry. 

### Issue 3

There is should be a good reason to have a `random` value in the production code. Choosing the retry intervals does not look like one of them. That can lead to unpredicted behaviors and not reproducible issues. 

## Proposal

1.  Change the default base in [_retry.json](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/data/_retry.json#L88) to the integer value of `0.01`, almost instant first retry should not be an issue.
2. Allow overriding default base using `botocore.Config`, similar to max_attempts in [_merge_client_retry_config](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/b54ceeaca5d4c1316fae0a0496422572d1f6aba5/botocore/translate.py#L41)

If contributors agree with that, I will be happy to make a change.

## Workaround

Currently, the only workaround I have found is to patch a client in a way like

```
firehose_client = boto3.client("firehose")
retry_handler = firehose_client.meta.events._unique_id_handlers["retry-config-firehose"]["handler"]
retry_handler._action.keywords["base"] = 0.05
```

issue_title: Q: arXiv spider: collaborations
issue_body:
## Question

can I raise a flag / create ticket / send email ... if there is something fishy when processing an xml in a spider?
I.e. chances are everything is OK, but a cataloger should have a closer look.
I don't want to crash it.

## Problem

`<author><keyname>:</keyname></author>`
comes in at least 2 varieties:
- everything before is a list of collaborations (if there are no affiliations)
- the 'author' before is a collaboration (if there are affiliations)

I can fix the spider to deal with both cases.

But I have no idea whether there are (will be) other cases.
And it's impossible to spot the name of a collaboration amongst several hundred authors if it is misidentified as author.
Therefore I would like to get a warning for records with author names ":"

Any other good idea is welcome.

## Example 1
http://export.arxiv.org/oai2?verb=GetRecord&identifier=oai:arXiv.org:1808.04927&metadataPrefix=arXiv 
``` xml
<author>
  <keyname>Zwaska</keyname>

issue_title: es-visitor: date bugfixing
issue_body:
So, it seems that date queries in ES are not working, as some records are missing some of the date fields that we are querying on. 
```
"range": {
    "date_field1": {
        "gt": "2017"
    },
    "date_field2": {
        "gt": "2017"
    },
    ...
}
```

Thus we need to generate a chain of `bool.should [ range1, range2, ... ]` kind of query. 
This is for `>, >=, <, <=` operators.

The tricky part would be the "equality", which comes from queries `d 2016` or `d = 2017-08`.
These have to be expressed in range queries, as well. 
For this, it will be helpful to look into `visitor_utils`. 

#### TODO
- [X] range queries with bool.should
- [x] range queries for equality 
- [x] wildcard queries
- [x] partial value
- [x] exact value

issue_title: Decimal values resetting
issue_body:
I have a simple model of an assessment and an assessment version.  One assessment can have multiple versions.  When I use the nested admin module the version control field is being reset on the UI to incrementing values starting at zero.  I can set the values through the django shell so the model and DB appear to be set up correctly.  I swapped back to the standard django admin and the values were stored and displayed as expected.  Any idea what could be causing the issue? 

DJANGO = 1.8 
django-nested-admin = 3.0.2

# Model
class Assessments(models.Model): 
    """Assessments Table"""
    Assessment = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=False)
    
    def __unicode__(self):
        return self.Assessment 
    
    class Meta: 
        ordering = ['Assessment']
        verbose_name_plural = "Assessments"
               
class AssessmentVersion(models.Model): 
    """Assessment Versions Table"""
    Assessment = models.ForeignKey('Assessments')
    Version = models.DecimalField(max_digits=4, decimal_places=2)
    Description = models.CharField(max_length=500, blank=True)
    
    def __unicode__(self): 
        return "{0} : {1}".format(self.Assessment, unicode(self.Version))

    class Meta: 
        ordering = ['Assessment','Version']
        verbose_name_plural="Assessment Version"
        

# Admin (swap for standard admin) 
class AssessmentVersionInLine(nested_admin.NestedStackedInline):
    model = AssessmentVersion 
    sortable_field_name = 'Version'
    extra = 0

class AssessmentAdmin(nested_admin.NestedModelAdmin):
    model = Assessments
    fieldsets = [
        (None,               {'fields': ['Assessment']}),
    ]
    sortable_field_name = 'Assessment'
    inlines = [AssessmentVersionInLine]
    extra = 0

issue_title: Define git branch for package from environment variable?
issue_body:
It it possible to define an environment variable to be used to determine the branch that should be checked out for a git repo used as a module? I have been trying to edit my Pipfile to include this but I'm not having any success. Here's an example error message:

pip9.exceptions.InstallationError: Command "git checkout -q ${PIPENV_ENVIRONMENT}" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-1kIzTE-export

The reason that I'm doing this is that I have two deployments (staging and master) and would like the packages that are pulled in to use the same branch as the repo that is pulling them in (so the master branch would pull in master packages and the staging branch would pull in staging packages).

issue_title: pkg_resources.Distribution should provide __dir__
issue_body: pkg_resources.Distribution uses `__getattr__` to proxy attributes to its _provider attribute; it should also implement its own `__dir__` so that (e.g.) tab-completion works correctly.

issue_title: pkg_resources.find_on_path() thinks the zc.recipe.egg wheel is an egg
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **mgedmin (Bitbucket: [mgedmin](http://bitbucket.org/mgedmin), GitHub: [mgedmin](http://github.com/mgedmin))**

---

Steps to reproduce: try to `pip install zc.recipe.egg`.  You'll get an error:

```
zc.recipe.egg is in an unsupported or invalid wheel
```

If you hack pip's sources to expose the real traceback, you'll see [this](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3028#issuecomment-148614984):

```
Traceback (most recent call last):                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/basecommand.py", line 200, in main                                                                                                                                                                       
    status = self.run(options, args)                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/commands/install.py", line 302, in run                                                                                                                                                                   
    root=options.root_path,                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/req/req_set.py", line 657, in install                                                                                                                                                                    
    **kwargs
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/req/req_install.py", line 805, in install
    version = pip.wheel.wheel_version(self.source_dir)
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/wheel.py", line 559, in wheel_version
    wheel_data = dist.get_metadata('WHEEL')
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/_vendor/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 1611, in get_metadata
    return self._get(self._fn(self.egg_info, name))
  File "/home/qwcode/p/pypa/pip/pip/_vendor/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 1722, in _get
    with open(path, 'rb') as stream:
IOError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/tmp/pip-build-ucnzjI/zc.recipe.egg/EGG-INFO/WHEEL'
```

AFAIU the problem is the following check in pkg_resources.find_on_path():

``` python
    if os.path.isdir(path_item) and os.access(path_item, os.R_OK):
        if path_item.lower().endswith('.egg'):
            # unpacked egg
```

This is wrong: the unpacked zc.recipe.egg _wheel_ has a top-level directory called `zc.recipe.egg`.

See https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3028 for more background.

---
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/462

issue_title: Python + Kafka community unification ideas
issue_body:
In the last couple of years there, we can’t help but notice that there has been quite a lot of fragmentation in the Python community around Kafka. As far as we can tell, there are three major projects as of this writing:

- [pykafka][pykafka-gh], this project, maintained by Emmett Butler and Keith Bourgoin from Parse.ly, with contributions by Yung-Chin Oei (pure Python with a `librdkafka` extension for speedups; evolved from Kafka 0.7 driver; has Kafka 0.8/0.9+ support)
- [kafka-python][], maintained by Dana Powers, currently at Pandora (pure Python, clean implementation focused on Kafka 0.9+)
- [confluent-kafka-python][], recently released by the Confluent team as part of the broader Kafka 0.10 release (Python C extension module wrapping `librdkafka`; Kafka 0.9+ focused)

[pykafka-gh]: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka
[kafka-python]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/kafka-python
[confluent-kafka-python]: https://github.com/confluentinc/confluent-kafka-python

Each project has a different history, level of current support for Kafka, and set of features — and, of course, different APIs. This is obviously not an ideal state for the Python user community around Kafka, both those that currently have Kafka clusters in production and for those looking to adopt Kafka for new projects.

I wonder if anyone has any ideas for what should happen -- if anything -- to unify the Python community around Kafka.

issue_title: BibKnowledge: get_kb_mappings_json not to return key if no value
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-05-08

Currently when using `get_kb_mappings_json()` for a key that has no value, the key is returned as value. For example:

```
 foobarKB:
    foo1 --> bar1
    foo2 -->
    foo3 --> bar3

  get_kb_mappings_json('foobarKB', 'foo2')
  {'label': 'foo2', 'value': 'foo2'}
```
source:modules/bibknowledge/lib/bibknowledge.py@a6221e6#L113

This is making impossible to disambiguate between the two cases:
```
foobarKB1:                      |            foobarKB2:
    foo1 --> bar1               |                foo1 --> bar1
    foo2 -->                    |                foo2 --> foo2
    foo3 --> bar3               |                foo3 --> bar3
```

One could be willing to receive `null` or empty string instead:
```
get_kb_mappings_json('foobarKB', 'foo2')
>>{'label': 'foo2', 'value': null}
```

or 


```
get_kb_mappings_json('foobarKB', 'foo2')
>>{'label': 'foo2', 'value': ""}
```

This function interface could also be extended similarly to [source:modules/bibformat/doc/hacking/bibformat-api.webdoc@1bb7957#L334 bibformat_engine.BibFormatObject.kb(kb, string, default="")] so that the default returned value can be decided:
```
get_kb_mappings_json('foobarKB', 'foo2', default="")
>>{'label': 'foo2', 'value': ""
get_kb_mappings_json('foobarKB', 'foo2', default='foo2')
>>{'label': 'foo2', 'value': 'foo2'}}
```

This might extend to other kb-related functions, and REST APIs.

(As briefly mused with Joe about one year ago, there is no reason not to move to this new interface)

issue_title: WebSearch: fix SPIRES search mode when terms contain quoted colons
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-09-28

The SPIRES search syntax mode does not work well for keyword searches
containing terms including colons.  The SQPP/SPIRES syntax parser is 
probably confused by the presence of colon, even though (i) the
colon is quoted and (i) the term before the colon is not valid logical field.  
The Invenio search syntax mode works well here.  Compare:

```
  keyword:"coupling: Yukawa"      ... good, 4117 records found
  find keyword "coupling: Yukawa" ... bad, 3 records found
  find keyword coupling: Yukawa   ... bad, 0 records found
```

issue_title: BibRank: possibility to disable citation losses check
issue_body:
`BibRank`'s citation indexer currently skips updating records for which the number of citations drops under a certain threshold.

It should be possible though, to disable this check, e.g. with a CLI flag such as `--disable-citation-losses-check` so that it's again possible to update those records for which a human has well verified that the citation drop has an justifiable explanation.

issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: Issue with the dollar sign ('$') character and get_data_yahoo()
issue_body:
Code for script `setup.py` that creates the error:
```python
import pandas_datareader.data as web

nasdaq_data = web.get_nasdaq_symbols()
symbols = nasdaq_data.index.values
web.get_data_yahoo('ABR-PA') #works
for x in symbols:
    print 'Getting data for: %s' % x
    web.get_data_yahoo(x) #throws RemoteDataError
```

Terminal output: 
```
Getting data for: A
Getting data for: AA
Getting data for: AAAP
Getting data for: AABA
...
Getting data for: ABR$A
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "../setup.py", line 23, in <module>
    web.get_data_yahoo(x)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/data.py", line 40, in get_data_yahoo
    return YahooDailyReader(*args, **kwargs).read()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/yahoo/daily.py", line 115, in read
    df = super(YahooDailyReader, self).read()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 181, in read
    params=self._get_params(self.symbols))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 79, in _read_one_data
    out = self._read_url_as_StringIO(url, params=params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 90, in _read_url_as_StringIO
    response = self._get_response(url, params=params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 139, in _get_response
    raise RemoteDataError('Unable to read URL: {0}'.format(url))
pandas_datareader._utils.RemoteDataError: Unable to read URL: https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v7/finance/download/ABR$A?crumb=nWOYHc6s101&interval=1d&period2=1504411199&events=history&period1=1262322000
```

There is incomplete compatibility between the method `get_nasdaq_symbols()` and the method `get_data_yahoo()`. NASDAQ represents option chains with a '^' on their website and with a '$' or '-' within the data returned by `get_nasdaq_symbols()`. Yahoo uses '-P' to represent the same character. 

The dollar sign is not a valid character in any symbol name. The `get_data_yahoo()` method should replace '$' with '-P' in any symbol name or list passed to it.

issue_title: On OSX qtpy applications are forcing discrete graphics
issue_body: Based on https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues/issues/3679 it seems that even when using the `Supports Automatic Graphics Switching` Option as described on https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17893849/qml-causing-switch-to-discrete-graphics, the applications seem to switch to discrete GPU. I created a test app on OSX based on a example. ## Using PyQt5 ```python # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """ ZetCode PyQt5 tutorial This example shows a tooltip on a window and a button. Author: Jan Bodnar Website: zetcode.com Last edited: August 2017 """ # Standard library impors import sys # Third party imports from PyQt5.QtGui import QFont from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QApplication, QPushButton, QToolTip, QWidget class Example(QWidget): def __init__(self): super(Example, self).__init__() self.initUI() def initUI(self): QToolTip.setFont(QFont('SansSerif', 10)) self.setToolTip('This is a QWidget widget') btn = QPushButton('Button', self) btn.setToolTip('This is a QPushButton widget') btn.resize(btn.sizeHint()) btn.move(50, 50) self.setGeometry(300, 300, 300, 200) self.setWindowTitle('Tooltips') self.show() def main(): app = QApplication(sys.argv) app.ex = Example() sys.exit(app.exec_()) if __name__ == '__main__': main() ``` ## Using qtpy ```python # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """ ZetCode PyQt5 tutorial This example shows a tooltip on a window and a button. Author: Jan Bodnar Website: zetcode.com Last edited: August 2017 """ # Standard library impors import sys # Third party imports from qtpy.QtGui import QFont from qtpy.QtWidgets import QApplication, QPushButton, QToolTip, QWidget class Example(QWidget): def __init__(self): super(Example, self).__init__() self.initUI() def initUI(self): QToolTip.setFont(QFont('SansSerif', 10)) self.setToolTip('This is a QWidget widget') btn = QPushButton('Button', self) btn.setToolTip('This is a QPushButton widget') btn.resize(btn.sizeHint()) btn.move(50, 50) self.setGeometry(300, 300, 300, 200) self.setWindowTitle('Tooltips') self.show() def main(): app = QApplication(sys.argv) app.ex = Example() sys.exit(app.exec_()) if __name__ == '__main__': main() ``` Results in (Notice the **Requires High Perf GPU** Column) This will affect any applications using `qtpy` on OSX, which results in higher energy consumption and hence decreasing battery life without need.

Any theory what is going on? Thanks.

issue_title: Improve test suite coverage of lensing modules
issue_body:
Our three [worst-covered](https://codecov.io/gh/GalSim-developers/GalSim/tree/20142beef4301bfd37f1cd035b7f9c16d1d3c3f4/galsim) (non-wfirst) files are currently (in order) pse.py, lensing_ps.py, and nfw_halo.py.

This issue is to address that by
1. adding more tests to cover functionality that is currently insufficiently tested.
2. removing some functionality that we probably don't need.  I'm thinking especially of the 4 different return values for many functions in lensing_ps.py.  Probably 2 would suffice -- a single value or a numpy array probably covers all the relevant use cases.
3. adding `# pragma: no cover` for some branches that might not actually need coverage.

I think all three files are related enough that they can be done as a joint effort, but anyone who takes this on can feel free to only do one or two of the files and leave the rest for someone else.

issue_title: Icon Request: icon-sort-alpha-up-alt for reversed
issue_body:
I love the [sort alpha](https://fontawesome.com/icons/sort-alpha-up?style=solid) icons, but I think it might be better in some situations to have a pair of icons where the A and Z reversed instead of the the arrow reversing.  For example, here is the sorting menu in Excel - notice the first icon is roughly the same as FA, but in the second the letters are swapped and the arrow points down.

![screen shot 2018-05-07 at 11 07 10 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/27421/39737563-6e853d9c-524b-11e8-9d19-b0ff939764f1.png)

I'm using the sort icons to show sorting in tables when the user clicks a header.  On first click, the A->Z icon is displayed and the contents sort from A to Z.  This looks great and the icon and the content match well.

When you click again to reverse, the *arrow* in the icon reverses and points up, but the A is still on top and the Z is still on the bottom.  The contents of the table, however, are the opposite: "Z" items are on top and "A" items are on the bottom.  The visual mismatch is actually pretty noticeable.

What I'm suggesting, even if only in Pro, is an alternate just for the reversed version of the icon ("up").  This would be paired with the existing "down" icons.

Thanks for the great work.

issue_title: patronictl pause does not go into effect inmediately
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:
- `patronictl pause` on a leader
- restart inmediately the Patroni service on it (i.e, to upgrade Patroni without causing a Postgres failover)

It seems the pause is not applied until the next time the HA loop (10s?) runs, so restarting Patroni before that happens causes Patroni to kill Postgres. The other nodes see the cluster as paused and will not do anything about it.

I haven't found the `paused` state exposed on the API so we can't query it to see if it was applied properly on all nodes - our workaround for now is sleeping some prudential time after the pause command was issued.

issue_title: toggling guake show/hide under wayland looses focus
issue_body:
Hi,

I am running Arch GNU/Linux with Gnome shell 3.16 on top of Wayland.
I have F12 set to show/hide Guake but when I run it under Wayland and press F12 Guake does not appear. This happens when I have no other apps opened. If I have any app opened and focused (for example Firefox) and press F12, Guake appears.

Another related issue is once Guake is opened, if I press F12 again to close it, it does not close. I need to press other keys and then F12 in order for it to work. 

Basically I cannot press F12/F12/F12.... to show/hide Guake. I managed to reproduce this on Arch with Gnome shell 3.14 and Wayland also, not only with 3.16. It runs perfectly on Xorg on both Gnome shell versions.

If you wish I can try to make a Vagrant box with GUI running a Fedora and I can make it easy for you to reproduce it.

issue_title: Store columns directly in TaxBenefitSystem
issue_body:
As discussed with @eraviart and @fpagnoux the idea is to store the columns directly in `TaxBenefitSystem` instead of entities.

Columns are generated by parsing Variable classes via metaclasses.

Currently the entities store the columns in [column_by_name](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/d62ff6b0eb3e81f4ceebdbee2d64e0c90848c20a/openfisca_core/entities.py#L8) and `TaxBenefitSystem` merges the dicts [here](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/d62ff6b0eb3e81f4ceebdbee2d64e0c90848c20a/openfisca_core/taxbenefitsystems.py#L45-L50)

issue_title: Hangs when target do not respond
issue_body:
In http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/advanced/#timeouts, it is said that an explicit timeout has to be passed into requests in order to get a timeout. Currently memento-client do not allow a timeout to be passed into [in any way](https://github.com/mementoweb/py-memento-client/blob/c7d3d1c35eba2e4dd219d01a5078bf5b18e923bb/memento_client/memento_client.py#L358). This causes issues when the server is is accessing do not respond:
```
$ strace python3 -c 'import datetime, memento_client; mc = memento_client.MementoClient(); mc.timegate_uri = "http://web.archive.org/web/"; mc.get_memento_info("http://www.icann.org/en/tlds/agreements/cat/cat-agreement-23sep05.htm", datetime.datetime.now())'
[...]
read(4, "\1\0\0\0\0\0\0\0\327\374U'nn\232\331\212n\35\331$\tM\nd\200\305[H\324=\272"..., 40) = 40
ioctl(4, FIONBIO, [0])                  = 0
write(4, "\27\3\3\0\331\232\217\f!\7\303u\241\375)F\365\334\332*\222\255O\22\272\201\252\226\274ru\203"..., 222) = 222
ioctl(4, FIONBIO, [0])                  = 0
read(4, 
```
`ioctl(4, FIONBIO, [0])` sets blocking network IO and `read(4,` will block for as long as we are concerned.

Please provide a default timeout or allow users to specify their own timeout.

(This is part of an [issue](https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T185561) reported downstream.)

issue_title: Implement that Travis-CI can run on branches.
Should we make Project a real thing in gcloud? Particular for gcloud.storage?
issue_body:
Our discussions in the past have said that we want `Connection` to remain stateless and really just handle sending data over the wire as well as authentication.

However, if you think about it, now that we're planning to push the "transformation" (from Python objects -> JSON) down to Buckets, Blobs, and ACLs, the connection object isn't really just doing transport, it's also representing a connection to a specific project.

Do you guys think it's worthwhile to make "project" a first-class citizen so that it makes more sense to do things like `project.get_all_buckets()` ?

This leads to a question of "what is the relationship with connections and projects? We could have a one-to-one between them (where what you really do is instantiate a project and under the hood it instantiates a connection which handles auth, etc). Or we could have a singleton connection that all the projects use with their own set of credentials...?

I am +0 on doing this kind of thing, and only just thought of it now given we need a "clean" place to put `get_all_buckets()` -- so feel free to close out as "stfu, that's dumb".

/cc @dhermes

issue_title: shadowsocks远程解析bug，翻墙网址会向本地dns提交dns解析，本地dns会返回错误的ip，从而会记录谁几时登陆了某网址！
issue_body:
我不清楚是shadowsocks还是SwitchyOmega的问题，所以向两位开发者都提交这个bug。

shadowsocks-dns远程解析bug，翻墙网址会向本地dns提交dns解析，本地dns会返回错误的ip，从而会记录谁几时登陆了某网址！
试验环境，
1，A机器xp，原版，
2，安装有wireshark，截获dns数据
3，本地设置114.114.114.114dns
4，chrome浏览器，安装SwitchyOmega，设置127.0.0.1:65500的socks5代理
5，shadowsocks代理。
截获步骤：
一、
1，无论设置SwitchyOmega自动代理，gfwed，登陆youtube.com，youtube可以打开，说明选择了正确的远程dns解析ip
2，wireshark，抓取数据包。
3，wireshark，分析中包含，有114返回的错误ip
![123](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10794158/5991448/3b712acc-aa2b-11e4-9299-921960c241c1.jpg)

结论：说明浏览器向远程ip和本地dns都发送了dns解析请求，但是只选择了远程的dns解析ip，但是同时本地的114上会有，某时间点本机所在外网ip请求了youtube。com的记录。如果倒查就爆菊了。

如果某人不想让某人知道自己上了某网址。那么这样就无法实现。
如果不想发送本地dns请求就要将dns设置成空，那么就不会去本地发送dns解析。但是问题又来了，如果在自动模式下，未翻墙网址就打不开了。

综上所述：
我的意思就是说能不能设置成翻墙dns解析走远程ip，强制不发送dns本地解析。谢谢开发者！

issue_title: Support for multiple versions of Python in same pex file
issue_body:
Use case: we want to distribute command-line tools written in Python to Linux users, and tell users "it'll work as long as you have Python 3.4, 3.5 or 3.6".

First prerequisite is manlinux1 wheels. Once that is supported we still have a problem, though: any package that has C extensions will result in `pex` file only working on version of Python it was built for. E.g. if pex was built with 3.4 and run with 3.5:

```console
$ PEX_VERBOSE=1 python3.5 dist/reloopd --help
pex: Found site-library: /usr/lib/python3.5/dist-packages
pex: Found site-library: /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages
pex: Found site-library: /usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages
pex: Tainted path element: /usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages
pex: Tainted path element: /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages
pex: Scrubbing from user site: /home/itamarst/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages
pex: Scrubbing from site-packages: /usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages
pex: Scrubbing from site-packages: /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages
pex: Failed to resolve a requirement: The 'Twisted==16.6.0' distribution was not found and is required by the application
pex: Failed to resolve a requirement: The 'PyYAML==3.12' distribution was not found and is required by the application
pex: Failed to resolve a requirement: The 'zope.interface==4.3.3' distribution was not found and is required by the application
pex: Unresolved requirements:
pex:   - zope.interface
pex:   - twisted
pex:   - pyyaml
pex: Distributions contained within this pex:
pex:   - Twisted-16.6.0-cp34-cp34m-linux_x86_64.whl
pex:   - PyYAML-3.12-cp34-cp34m-linux_x86_64.whl
pex:   - reloop-0+untagged.14.g07067a9.dirty-py3-none-any.whl
pex:   - click-6.7-py2.py3-none-any.whl
pex:   - constantly-15.1.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
pex:   - setuptools-32.3.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl
pex:   - zope.interface-4.3.3-cp34-cp34m-linux_x86_64.whl
pex:   - incremental-16.10.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Failed to execute PEX file, missing compatible dependencies for:
zope.interface

```

The only solution AFAICT is to support including wheels for multiple versions of Python (3.4, 3.5, 3.6).

issue_title: for non-test-related pytest failures, pants still tries to parse junit.xml
issue_body:
On Pants v1.3.0, in `src/python/pants/backend/python/tasks2/pytest_run.py`, the `_run_pytest` method will invoke the `_get_failed_targets_from_junitxml` method regardless of whether pex got far enough to even invoke tests. This results in an irrelevant error message that pants had an "Error parsing xml file" when in fact the subprocess invocation never got that far to even contemplate generating the junit XML output.

For example, if pex fails to find pytest, then pytest will never be invoked. The junit XML output won't exist in this case.

issue_title: Invalid permissions on Lambda function
issue_body:
I got an `Invalid permissions on Lambda function` when I try to invoke my lambda function from API Gateway.

In fact SAM generate this invalid arn in API Gateway Lambda trigger :
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1262860/36101957-168eb96a-100b-11e8-9b75-a6cf43977f34.png)

`ANY` is not valid in this arn.
Reference: [Resource Format of Permissions for Executing API in API Gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-control-access-using-iam-policies-to-invoke-api.html#api-gateway-iam-policy-resource-format-for-executing-api)

This notation is the same than [API swagger cors example](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/examples/2016-10-31/api_swagger_cors/template.yaml)

```yaml
Resources:
  ApiGateway:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: prod
      DefinitionBody:
        swagger: '2.0'
        info:
          title: "Example API"
          version: "v0.1.0"
        host: "https://example.com"
        basePath: "/"
        schemes:
          - https
        paths:
          "/{proxy+}":
            x-amazon-apigateway-any-method:
              x-amazon-apigateway-integration:
                # https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-control-access-using-iam-policies-to-invoke-api.html
                uri:
                  Fn::Sub: arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${LambdaFunction.Arn}/invocations
                httpMethod: POST
                type: aws_proxy
              consumes:
                - application/json
              produces:
                - application/json
              parameters:
                - name: proxy
                  in: path
                  required: true
                  type: string
              responses:
                "200":
                  schema:
                    $ref: '#/definitions/Empty'
  LambdaFunction:
    Type: 'AWS::Serverless::Function'
    Properties:
      FunctionName: "ExampleFunction"
      Handler: main.handler
      Runtime: nodejs6.10
      MemorySize: 512 # 512 Mo
      CodeUri: ../dist
      Events:
        SendEmailEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref ApiGateway
            Path: /{proxy+}
            Method: ANY
```

Workaround:
```yaml
      Events:
        SendEmailEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref ApiGateway
            Path: /{proxy+}
            Method: "*"
```

Related to https://stackoverflow.com/questions/41817075/cloudformation-invalid-permissions-on-lambda-function#41819244

CC @yvele

issue_title: Feature Request: Optional timeout override on a per-call basis
issue_body:
Currently we can configure the timeout used for the Plaid client as a whole by passing in a setting when the client is created. All calls made with that client will then use that timeout.

In some cases it is desirable to have different timeouts for different kinds of operations. It allows "fast" operations to time out quickly if something is wrong, while letting "slow" ones take all the time they need. It would therefore be helpful to have the option to set timeouts on a per-call basis.

This might look like an optional parameter to every API call (e.g. by default `timeout=None`), which, if set, would override the globally-configured default value. If not set, the usual default would be used.

Thanks for considering it!

issue_title: play_from_queue() returns None instead of True
issue_body:
The docstring of `play_from_queue()` states
```
Returns:
    True if the Sonos speaker successfully started playing the track.
    False if the track did not start (this may be because it was not
    requested to start because "start=False")
```
However, it currently returns None if `start=True` and False if `start=False`.

There is no reason for `play_from_queue()` to return a success indicator: If it fails, it will raise an exception.

IMO, we should remove the return value. What do you think?

issue_title: ignoreHttp1xx or ignoreHttp1Xx
issue_body:
Hi, first I would like to thank you for this lib, It is been useful for us here.

Said that, I would like to discuss about a possible bug I've found. But before sending my pull request I want to discuss  the best solution to fix it.

I notice that the attribute "ignoreHttp1xx" of the "healthCheck" is been converted to "ignoreHttp1Xx" with the first "X" in capital letter. Based on the Marathon scheme referenced in this link https://mesosphere.github.io/marathon/docs/generated/api.html#v2_apps_post  it is a boolean and the atribute "ignoreHttp1xx" with "x" in lower case. 

However marathon accepts the atribute with capital "X". I'm using marathon version 0.13.0

After analysing the results, I look into the library code and realized that the problem is in the native python string method "w.tittle()" line https://github.com/thefactory/marathon-python/blob/master/marathon/util.py#L59  

I've developed one possible solution rewriting the method title and it is like this :

https://github.com/trsouz/marathon-python/commit/ddcc9476941b821ede7a34ae679018c730aefda3

As marathon also accepts the wrong attribute, this is generating a bug in fact. I'm creating an application that stores and validate the marathon atributes according with their scheme and it's generating a bug in my application because I'm having to create a validation too specific only for this atribute. 

A simple way to see this behaviour is with this python code.

. 'httpxx'.title() and then  'http1xx'.title()

Thanks
